scenario: Harbor Bank routes 7000 transactions per day through a weekday-only batch in Camunda 8.8. A FEEL expression on an exclusive gateway condition must read the day name from the booked date and compare it to a literal weekday list, using the documented day of week function on the date value 2019-09-17.
question: What does the FEEL day of week function return for the input date 2019-09-17?

options.text: It returns the integer 2 because the Gregorian calendar numbers the days of the week starting at 1 for Sunday and the input is the third day of the week
It returns the localized day name in the language configured at the cluster level, so the result varies by deployment for the same input date value
It returns the ISO-8601 ordinal date code formatted as 2019-W38-2 and lets the caller derive the day name from the trailing digit of the resulting string
It returns the English name of the day as a string, so the documented call on 2019-09-17 yields the value Tuesday in line with the temporal functions reference

a.text: Incorrect. The function returns a string rather than an integer; the temporal functions reference shows the result is the English day name.

b.text: Incorrect. The documented function always returns the English name of the day regardless of any cluster locale configuration.

c.text: Incorrect. ISO week dates are produced by other temporal functions; day of week returns a plain English day name as a string.

d.text: Correct. The temporal functions reference documents that day of week returns the English name of the day, so the call on 2019-09-17 yields Tuesday.

explanation: FEEL day of week takes a date or date and time value and returns the English name of the day of the week as a string, regardless of any locale configuration.
